```{r}
# should show 2024-11-01 … 2024-11-30 in the date column and the real SF Tmax.

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# (D) Now modify the ggplot that you had for December 2024, e.g.:

library(ggplot2)

ggplot() +
  # (1) Plot the 30‐day “history” in grey (solid line)
  geom_line(
    data = df_prev,
    aes(x = date, y = tmax),
    color     = "grey50",
    size      = 0.8
  ) +
  # (2) 95% CI ribbon for Dec 2024 (as before)
  geom_ribbon(
    data = df_interval_hold,
    aes(x    = date,
        ymin = lower,
        ymax = upper,
        fill = series),
    alpha = 0.3
  ) +
  # (3) Actual Dec 2024 line (solid, dark orange)
  geom_line(
    data = filter(df_af_hold, type == "actual"),
    aes(x = date, y = value, color = series, linetype = type),
    size = 0.8
  ) +
  # (4) Forecast Dec 2024 line (dashed, dark orange)
  geom_line(
    data = filter(df_af_hold, type == "fit"),
    aes(x = date, y = value, color = series, linetype = type),
    size = 0.8
  ) +
  # (5) Colour/Fill scales (only “tmax” series)
  scale_color_manual(
    name   = "Series",
    values = c(tmax = "#D85711"),
    labels = c(tmax = "Tmax")
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    name   = "Series",
    values = c(tmax = "#D85711"),
    labels = c(tmax = "Tmax")
  ) +
  # (6) Linetype legend: solid = actual/past, dashed = forecast
  scale_linetype_manual(
    name   = "Type",
    values = c(
      actual = "solid",
      fit    = "dashed"
    ),
    labels = c(
      actual = "Actual",
      fit    = "Forecast"
    )
  ) +
  # (7) Axis labels + title
  labs(
    x     = "Date",
    y     = "Tmax (°C)",
    title = "SF Downtown: Nov 2024 History + December 2024 Forecast of Tmax",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "Historical (Nov 1-Nov 30) in grey; ",
      "Out‐of‐Sample Dec 1-Dec 31 with 95% CI ribbon"
    )
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title     = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle  = element_text(size = 12, face = "italic"),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )

```






```{r}
#| eval: false
#| include: false


# 29) Plot last 60 days of training (before Dec 2024), actual Dec 2024, and Dec 2024 forecasts for SF

# (a) find the index of "2024-10-31" within all_dates
last_train_date <- as.Date("2024-10-31")
last_train_idx  <- which(all_dates == last_train_date) 
start_plot      <- max(1, last_train_idx - 60)

# (b) build the two vectors *using the same index range*
date_seq <- all_dates[start_plot:last_train_idx]
temp_seq <- sf_true_train[start_plot:last_train_idx]

train_last60_df <- tibble(
  date = date_seq,
  temp = temp_seq,
  type = "Observed (Train)"
)

# Data frame for actual December 2024
dec2024_dates <- all_dates[test_idx]
actual_dec_df <- tibble(
  date = dec2024_dates,
  temp = sf_true_test,
  type = "Observed (Dec 2024)"
)

# Data frame for December 2024 forecasts
forecast_dec_df <- tibble(
  date = dec2024_dates,
  temp = sf_fore_test,
  type = "Forecast (Dec 2024)"
)

# Combine all three
plot_df <- bind_rows(train_last60_df, actual_dec_df, forecast_dec_df)

# 30) Plot everything with 90% prediction interval for Dec 2024
ribbon_dec_df <- tibble(
  date  = dec2024_dates,
  lower = sf_lower_test,
  upper = sf_upper_test
)
```

#### 3.4.2.a

```{r}
#| eval: false
#| include: false
# (Assumes Y_train, filter_res_train, sf_hat_train, and all_dates/train_idx are already defined)

# 1) Extract in‐sample residuals for each station
resid_matrix <- Y_train - filter_res_train$f   # [n_train × 4]
colnames(resid_matrix) <- station_names

# 2) Convert residuals to ts objects (optional)
dates_train <- all_dates[train_idx]
resid_ts <- lapply(1:ncol(resid_matrix), function(j) {
  ts(resid_matrix[, j],
     start = c(year(dates_train[1]), yday(dates_train[1])),
     frequency = 365)
})
names(resid_ts) <- station_names

# 3) Choose SF residuals for diagnostics
sf_resid <- resid_matrix[, "SAN FRANCISCO DWTN"]

# 4) Time‐series plot of SF residuals
plot(dates_train, sf_resid, type = "l", col = "darkgray",
     main = "SF In‐Sample Residuals over Time",
     xlab = "Date", ylab = "Residual (°C)")

# 5) Histogram + density overlay
hist(sf_resid, breaks = 30, freq = FALSE,
     main = "Histogram of SF Residuals (Train)",
     xlab = "Residual (°C)", col = "lightblue")
lines(density(sf_resid, na.rm = TRUE), col = "darkblue", lwd = 2)

# 6) QQ‐plot vs Normal
qqnorm(sf_resid, main = "QQ‐Plot of SF Residuals (Train)")
qqline(sf_resid, col = "red", lwd = 2)

# 7) ACF and PACF of SF residuals
par(mar = c(4, 4, 2, 1))
acf(sf_resid, na.action = na.pass, main = "ACF of SF Residuals")
pacf(sf_resid, na.action = na.pass, main = "PACF of SF Residuals")
par(mfrow = c(1,1))

# 8) Ljung-Box test for autocorrelation at various lags
for (L in c(7, 14, 21, 28)) {
  lb <- Box.test(sf_resid, lag = L, type = "Ljung-Box", fitdf = 0)
  cat(sprintf("Ljung-Box (lag=%d):  statistic=%.3f, p‐value=%.3f\n",
              L, lb$statistic, lb$p.value))
}

# 9) Kolmogorov-Smirnov test for normality (since Shapiro‐Wilk fails for large n)
sf_resid2 <- na.omit(sf_resid)
ks <- ks.test(sf_resid2, "pnorm",
              mean = mean(sf_resid2),
              sd   = sd(sf_resid2))
cat(sprintf("KS test: statistic=%.3f, p‐value=%.3f\n", ks$statistic, ks$p.value))

# 10) Residuals vs Fitted plot
sf_fitted <- sf_hat_train
valid_idx_train <- which(!is.na(sf_resid))
plot(sf_fitted[valid_idx_train], sf_resid[valid_idx_train],
     xlab = "Fitted Value (°C)", ylab = "Residual (°C)",
     main = "SF Residuals vs Fitted", col = "darkgray", pch = 16)
abline(h = 0, col = "red", lwd = 2)

# 11) Repeat ACF/PACF and Ljung-Box for each station
for (j in seq_along(station_names)) {
  stn <- station_names[j]
  resid_j <- resid_matrix[, j]
  cat("\n--- Diagnostics for station:", stn, "---\n")
  acf(resid_j, na.action = na.pass, main = paste("ACF of", stn, "Residuals"))
  pacf(resid_j, na.action = na.pass, main = paste("PACF of", stn, "Residuals"))
  lbj <- Box.test(resid_j, lag = 14, type = "Ljung-Box", fitdf = 0)
  cat(sprintf("Ljung-Box (lag=14) for %s: statistic=%.3f, p‐value=%.3f\n",
              stn, lbj$statistic, lbj$p.value))
}

# 12) Summary statistics for each station’s residuals
for (j in seq_along(station_names)) {
  stn <- station_names[j]
  resid_j <- resid_matrix[, j]
  cat(sprintf("\nStation %s: mean=%.4f, sd=%.4f, missing%%=%.2f\n",
              stn,
              mean(resid_j, na.rm = TRUE),
              sd(resid_j, na.rm = TRUE),
              100 * sum(is.na(resid_j)) / length(resid_j)))
}

# 13) Residual heatmap (optional)
library(reshape2)
resid_df <- data.frame(date = dates_train, resid_matrix)
resid_melt <- melt(resid_df, id.vars = "date", variable.name = "Station", value.name = "Residual")
ggplot(resid_melt, aes(x = date, y = Station, fill = Residual)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "blue", mid = "white", high = "red", midpoint = 0) +
  labs(title = "Residuals Heatmap (Train)", x = "Date", y = "Station") +
  theme_minimal()

```

### 3.4.3. TMIN

```{r}
#| eval: false
#| include: false
# Install and load required packages
# CHANGED: removed install.packages() — packages should already be installed

library(dlm)         # for Dynamic Linear Models
library(tidyverse)   # for data manipulation (dplyr, tidyr, ggplot2, etc.)
library(lubridate)   # for working with Dates
library(geosphere)   # for distance computations

# 1) Read the GHCN CSV
ghcn <- read.csv("fp_data/ghcn.txt", header = TRUE)
colnames(ghcn) <- c(
  "station_id",
  "station_name",
  "latitude",
  "longitude",
  "elevation_m",
  "date",
  "tmin_tenthC",
  "tmax_tenthC",
  "tavg_tenthC",
  "prcp_tenthmm"
)

# 2) Convert types, compute Celsius/mm, drop "_tenth" fields
ghcn <- ghcn %>%
  mutate(
    date = as_date(date),          # YYYY-MM-DD → Date
    tmin = tmin_tenthC / 10,       # tenths °C → °C
    tmax = tmax_tenthC / 10,       # tenths °C → °C
    prcp = prcp_tenthmm / 10       # tenths mm → mm
  ) %>%
  select(
    station_id, station_name, latitude, longitude, elevation_m,
    date, tmin, tmax, prcp
  )

# 3) Restrict to dates ≥ 1976-11-01
ghcn <- filter(ghcn, date >= as.Date("1976-11-01"))

# 4) Subset to four stations
target_stations <- c(
  "SAN FRANCISCO DWTN",
  "PASO ROBLES MUNI AP",
  "BAKER CITY AP",
  "PRESCOTT LOVE FLD"
)
ghcn_sub <- filter(ghcn, station_name %in% target_stations)

# 5) Pivot to wide format for Tmin
tmin_wide <- ghcn_sub %>%
  select(date, station_name, tmin) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = station_name, values_from = tmin) %>%
  arrange(date)

# 6) Extract matrix of Tmin values
Y_min <- as.matrix(tmin_wide[,-1])
rownames(Y_min) <- tmin_wide$date

# 7) Dates vector
all_dates <- as.Date(tmin_wide$date)

# 8) Define train/test split:
#    train = all dates < 2024-12-01; test = dates in December 2024
train_idx <- which(all_dates < as.Date("2024-12-01"))
test_idx  <- which(all_dates >= as.Date("2024-12-01") & all_dates < as.Date("2025-01-01"))

# 9) Reorder columns so SF is first
station_names <- colnames(Y_min)
sf_idx <- which(station_names == "SAN FRANCISCO DWTN")
new_order <- c(sf_idx, setdiff(seq_along(station_names), sf_idx))
Y_min <- Y_min[ , new_order]
station_names <- station_names[new_order]

# 10) Station coordinates in same order
station_coords <- ghcn_sub %>%
  select(station_name, latitude, longitude) %>%
  distinct() %>%
  slice(match(station_names, station_name))

# 11) Compute distances (km) from SF
sf_coord <- station_coords %>%
  filter(station_name == "SAN FRANCISCO DWTN") %>%
  select(longitude, latitude) %>%
  as.numeric()
coords_mat <- station_coords %>% select(longitude, latitude) %>% as.matrix()
dist_m <- distHaversine(coords_mat, sf_coord)
dist_km <- dist_m / 1000

# 12) Spatially varying loadings λ_j = exp(-d_j / ρ)
rho <- 500
lambda <- exp(- dist_km / rho)  # length = 4

# 13) Build base DLM for latent factor (trend + seasonal)
base_trend <- dlmModPoly(order = 2, dV = 0, dW = c(1e-7, 1e-7))
base_season <- dlmModTrig(s = 365, q = 2, dV = 0, dW = 1e-5)
base_model <- base_trend + base_season
m_base <- ncol(base_model$FF)   # should be 6

# 14) Observation matrix F_spatial (4 × 6): each row = λ_j * base_model$FF
n_stations <- length(lambda)    # 4
F_spatial <- matrix(0, nrow = n_stations, ncol = m_base)
for (j in 1:n_stations) {
  F_spatial[j, ] <- lambda[j] * base_model$FF
}

# 15) Transition matrix G_spatial (6 × 6)
G_spatial <- base_model$GG

# 16) Templates for W & V (we will replace W[1,1] and diag(V))
W_template <- base_model$W
V_template <- diag(1, n_stations)

# 17) Prior for factor state
m0_spatial <- rep(0, m_base)
C0_spatial <- diag(1e6, m_base)

# 18) Builder function for MLE on training data
buildSpatialModel <- function(param) {
  W_level   <- exp(param[1])
  V_obs_raw <- exp(param[2])
  V_obs     <- min(V_obs_raw, 0.5)

  Wt <- W_template
  Wt[1,1] <- W_level
  Vt <- diag(V_obs, n_stations)

  dlm(
    m0 = m0_spatial,
    C0 = C0_spatial,
    FF = F_spatial,
    V  = Vt,
    GG = G_spatial,
    W  = Wt
  )
}

# 19) Split Y_min into training and testing sets
Y_train <- Y_min[train_idx, ]
Y_test  <- Y_min[test_idx, ]

# 20) MLE on Y_train
init_param <- log(c(W_level = 0.1, V_obs = 1))
mle_fit_spatial <- dlmMLE(Y_train, parm = init_param, build = buildSpatialModel)
stopifnot(mle_fit_spatial$convergence == 0)

opt_param_spatial <- exp(mle_fit_spatial$par)
names(opt_param_spatial) <- c("W_level", "V_obs")
print(opt_param_spatial)

# 21) Rebuild fitted model on training data
mod_spatial_fitted <- buildSpatialModel(mle_fit_spatial$par)

# 22) Kalman filtering on training data
filter_res_train <- dlmFilter(Y_train, mod_spatial_fitted)

# 23) One‐step ahead forecasts on train
y_hat_train <- filter_res_train$f                # [n_train × 4]
sf_hat_train  <- y_hat_train[, 1]                 # SF predictions
sf_true_train <- Y_train[, 1]                     # SF actuals

# 24) Within‐sample accuracy (RMSE, MAE) for SF
resid_train <- sf_true_train - sf_hat_train
valid_idx_train <- which(!is.na(resid_train))
rmse_train <- sqrt(mean((sf_true_train[valid_idx_train] - sf_hat_train[valid_idx_train])^2))
mae_train  <- mean(abs(sf_true_train[valid_idx_train] - sf_hat_train[valid_idx_train]))
cat("Within‐sample (train) SF RMSE =", round(rmse_train, 3), "\n")
cat("Within‐sample (train) SF MAE  =", round(mae_train, 3), "\n")

# 25) Forecast on test (December 2024)
n_test <- nrow(Y_test)
forecast_res_test <- dlmForecast(filter_res_train, nAhead = n_test)

# 26) Extract SF forecast for test
if (is.list(forecast_res_test$f)) {
  Y_fore_test_mat <- do.call(rbind, forecast_res_test$f)
} else {
  Y_fore_test_mat <- forecast_res_test$f
}
sf_fore_test <- Y_fore_test_mat[, 1]
sf_true_test <- Y_test[, 1]

# 27) Out‐of‐sample accuracy (RMSE, MAE) for SF
valid_idx_test <- which(!is.na(sf_true_test) & !is.na(sf_fore_test))
sf_true_test2 <- sf_true_test[valid_idx_test]
sf_fore_test2 <- sf_fore_test[valid_idx_test]

rmse_test <- sqrt(mean((sf_true_test2 - sf_fore_test2)^2))
mae_test  <- mean(abs(sf_true_test2 - sf_fore_test2))
cat("Out‐of‐sample (Dec 2024) SF RMSE =", round(rmse_test, 3), "\n")
cat("Out‐of‐sample (Dec 2024) SF MAE  =", round(mae_test, 3), "\n")


```

```{r}
#| eval: false
#| include: false
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# (A) Extract the one-step forecast SD (√Var) for SF (first state) on each Dec day:
sf_sd_test_min <- sapply(forecast_res_test$Q, function(Sig) sqrt(Sig[1,1]))

# (B) 95% quantile of the standard normal:
z95 <- qnorm(0.975)   # ≈ 1.96

# (C) 95% lower/upper bounds for SF Tmin forecasts (length = 31 for Dec 2024):
sf_lower95_test_min <- sf_fore_test - z95 * sf_sd_test_min
sf_upper95_test_min <- sf_fore_test + z95 * sf_sd_test_min

```

```{r}
#| eval: false
#| include: false
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# (D) Compute the 95% half-width for each of the 31 hold-out days:
half_width95_min <- (sf_upper95_test_min - sf_lower95_test_min) / 2

# (E) Summary of those half-widths:
summary(half_width95_min)   # ← RUN THIS; you’ll get Min, 1st Qu., Median, Mean, 3rd Qu., Max

```

```{r}
#| eval: false
#| include: false
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
library(tibble)

# (F) Grab the 31 hold-out dates (2024-12-01 … 2024-12-31):
hold_dates_min <- all_dates[test_idx]  # should be Dec 1, 2024 through Dec 31, 2024

# (G) Assemble df_hold_min
df_hold_min <- tibble(
  date        = hold_dates_min,
  actual_tmin = as.numeric(sf_true_test),         # actual SF Tmin for Dec 2024
  fit_tmin    = as.numeric(sf_fore_test),         # forecasted SF Tmin
  lower_tmin  = as.numeric(sf_lower95_test_min),
  upper_tmin  = as.numeric(sf_upper95_test_min)
)

# (H) Inspect it:
print(df_hold_min)   # ← RUN THIS; you should see 31 rows × 5 columns

```


```{r}
#| eval: false
#| include: false
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# (I) Interval ribbon (one row per date, with columns date, lower, upper, series):
df_interval_hold_min <- df_hold_min %>%
  select(date, lower_tmin, upper_tmin) %>%
  rename(lower = lower_tmin, upper = upper_tmin) %>%
  mutate(series = "tmin")

# Check:
print(df_interval_hold_min)
# Should have 31 rows: date | lower | upper | series="tmin"

# (J) Actual vs. forecast (long format; two rows per date):
df_af_hold_min <- df_hold_min %>%
  select(date, actual_tmin, fit_tmin) %>%
  rename(actual = actual_tmin, fit = fit_tmin) %>%
  mutate(series = "tmin") %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = c("actual", "fit"),
    names_to  = "type",
    values_to = "value"
  )

# Check:
print(df_af_hold_min)
# Should have 62 rows: for each date, one row type="actual", one row type="fit".

```


```{r}
#| eval: false
#| include: false
library(ggplot2)

ggplot() +
  # (a) 95% CI ribbon (fill by series="tmin")
  geom_ribbon(
    data = df_interval_hold_min,
    aes(x    = date,
        ymin = lower,
        ymax = upper,
        fill = series),
    alpha = 0.3
  ) +
  # (b) Actual SF Tmin (solid line)
  geom_line(
    data = filter(df_af_hold_min, type == "actual"),
    aes(x = date, y = value, color = series, linetype = type),
    size = 0.8
  ) +
  # (c) Forecast SF Tmin (dashed line)
  geom_line(
    data = filter(df_af_hold_min, type == "fit"),
    aes(x = date, y = value, color = series, linetype = type),
    size = 0.8
  ) +
  # (d) Colour/Fill scales (single series = "tmin", dark orange)
  scale_color_manual(
    name   = "Series",
    values = c(tmin = "steelblue"),
    labels = c(tmin = "Tmin")
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    name   = "Series",
    values = c(tmin = "steelblue"),
    labels = c(tmin = "Tmin")
  ) +
  # (e) Linetype legend: solid = actual, dashed = fit
  scale_linetype_manual(
    name   = "Type",
    values = c(actual = "solid", fit = "dashed"),
    labels = c(actual = "Actual", fit = "Forecast")
  ) +
  labs(
    x     = "Date",
    y     = "Tmin (°C)",
    title = "SF Downtown: December 2024 One-Step-Ahead Forecast of Tmin",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "Out-of-Sample (31-Day) Forecast: ",
      format(min(hold_dates_min), "%Y-%m-%d"), " to ",
      format(max(hold_dates_min), "%Y-%m-%d")
    )
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title     = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle  = element_text(size = 12, face = "italic"),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )

```


```{r}
#| eval: false
#| include: false
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# (K) Find the first hold-out index, then grab the 30 days before it:
first_test_idx_min <- min(test_idx)          # index of 2024-12-01 in all_dates
prev_idx_min      <- (first_test_idx_min - 30):(first_test_idx_min - 1)

# Sanity check your dates:
# head(all_dates[prev_idx_min], 3)  # → 2024-11-01, 2024-11-02, ...
# tail(all_dates[prev_idx_min], 3)  # → 2024-11-28, 2024-11-29, 2024-11-30

prev_dates_min <- all_dates[prev_idx_min]

# (L) Extract the “true” SF Tmin for those 30 days (SF is column 1 of Y_min):
prev_tmin <- Y_min[prev_idx_min, 1]

# (M) Build a tibble of the 30-day history:
df_prev_min <- tibble(
  date = prev_dates_min,
  tmin = as.numeric(prev_tmin)
)

# Check:
# print(df_prev_min)  # should show 2024-11-01 through 2024-11-30 and real SF Tmin

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# (N) Now re-plot everything, but first draw the November line (grey):
ggplot() +
  # (1) Grey line for the 30 days of observed SF Tmin (Nov 1–30, 2024)
  geom_line(
    data = df_prev_min,
    aes(x = date, y = tmin),
    color = "grey50",
    size  = 0.8
  ) +
  # (2) 95% CI ribbon for Dec 2024
  geom_ribbon(
    data = df_interval_hold_min,
    aes(x    = date,
        ymin = lower,
        ymax = upper,
        fill = series),
    alpha = 0.3
  ) +
  # (3) Actual SF Tmin for Dec 2024 (solid line)
  geom_line(
    data = filter(df_af_hold_min, type == "actual"),
    aes(x = date, y = value, color = series, linetype = type),
    size = 0.8
  ) +
  # (4) Forecast SF Tmin for Dec 2024 (dashed line)
  geom_line(
    data = filter(df_af_hold_min, type == "fit"),
    aes(x = date, y = value, color = series, linetype = type),
    size = 0.8
  ) +
  # (5) Colour/Fill for series "tmin"
  scale_color_manual(
    name   = "Series",
    values = c(tmin = "steelblue"),
    labels = c(tmin = "Tmin")
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    name   = "Series",
    values = c(tmin = "steelblue"),
    labels = c(tmin = "Tmin")
  ) +
  # (6) Linetype legend: solid = actual/past, dashed = forecast
  scale_linetype_manual(
    name   = "Type",
    values = c(actual = "solid", fit = "dashed"),
    labels = c(actual = "Actual", fit = "Forecast")
  ) +
  labs(
    x     = "Date",
    y     = "Tmin (°C)",
    title = "SF Downtown: Nov 2024 History + Dec 2024 Forecast of Tmin",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "Historical (Nov 1–Nov 30) in grey; ",
      "Out-of-Sample Dec 1–Dec 31 with 95 % CI ribbon"
    )
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title     = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle  = element_text(size = 12, face = "italic"),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )

```


```{r}
#| eval: false
#| include: false
#| fig-height: 6
#| fig-width: 12


library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)

# 1) Re‐shape “Nov 2024 history” for Tmax and Tmin into a common format:
df_prev_tmax2 <- df_prev %>%
  rename(value = tmax) %>%         # rename tmax → value
  mutate(
    series = "tmax",               # tag as “tmax”
    type   = "history"             # tag as “history”
  ) %>%
  select(date, series, type, value)

df_prev_tmin2 <- df_prev_min %>%
  rename(value = tmin) %>%         # rename tmin → value
  mutate(
    series = "tmin",               # tag as “tmin”
    type   = "history"             # tag as “history”
  ) %>%
  select(date, series, type, value)

df_prev_all <- bind_rows(df_prev_tmax2, df_prev_tmin2)
# df_prev_all has columns: date | series ("tmax"/"tmin") | type="history" | value

# 2) Stack the two “Dec 2024 95 % CI” data.frames:
df_interval_hold_all <- bind_rows(
  df_interval_hold_min %>% select(date, lower, upper, series),
  df_interval_hold     %>% select(date, lower, upper, series)
)
# now df_interval_hold_all has columns: date | lower | upper | series ("tmin"/"tmax")

# 3) Stack the two “Dec 2024 actual vs. fit” data.frames:
df_af_hold_all <- bind_rows(
  df_af_hold_min %>% select(date, series, type, value),
  df_af_hold     %>% select(date, series, type, value)
)
# df_af_hold_all has: date | series ("tmin"/"tmax") | type ∈ {actual, fit} | value

# 4) One‐panel ggplot putting everything together:
spatial_forecast <- ggplot() +
  # (a) Nov 2024 history (solid lines for both series)
  geom_line(
    data = df_prev_all,
    aes(x = date, y = value, color = series, linetype = type),
    size = 0.7
  ) +
  # (b) Dec 2024 95 % CI ribbons (one ribbon per series)
  geom_ribbon(
    data = df_interval_hold_all,
    aes(x = date, ymin = lower, ymax = upper, fill = series),
    alpha = 0.25
  ) +
  # (c) Dec 2024 actuals (solid lines; these appear only on Dec dates)
  geom_line(
    data = filter(df_af_hold_all, type == "actual"),
    aes(x = date, y = value, color = series, linetype = type),
    size = 0.8
  ) +
  # (d) Dec 2024 forecasts (dashed lines)
  geom_line(
    data = filter(df_af_hold_all, type == "fit"),
    aes(x = date, y = value, color = series, linetype = type),
    size = 0.8
  ) +
  # (e) Color/fill scales: steelblue for Tmin; darkorange for Tmax
  scale_color_manual(
    name   = "Series",
    values = c(tmin = "steelblue", tmax = "#D85711"),
    labels = c(tmin = "Tmin", tmax = "Tmax")
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    name   = "Series",
    values = c(tmin = "steelblue", tmax = "#D85711"),
    labels = c(tmin = "Tmin", tmax = "Tmax")
  ) +
  # (f) Linetype legend: history=solid, actual=solid, fit=dashed
  scale_linetype_manual(
    name   = "Type",
    values = c(
      history = "solid",
      actual  = "solid",
      fit     = "dashed"
    ),
    labels = c(
      history = "Historical (Nov 2024)",
      actual  = "Actual (Dec 2024)",
      fit     = "Forecast (Dec 2024)"
    )
  ) +
  # (g) Labels and theme
  labs(
    x        = "Date",
    y        = "Temperature (°C)",
    title    = "SF Downtown: Nov 2024 History & Dec 2024 Forecasts for Tmin & Tmax",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "Nov 1–30 2024 in solid; Dec 1–31 2024 forecasts with 95 % CI ribbon"
    )
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title    = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11, face = "italic"),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )
print(spatial_forecast)
ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "spatial_forecast.pdf"), plot = spatial_forecast, device = "pdf", width = 12, height = 6, units = "in")
```


#### 3.4.3.a


```{r}
#| eval: false
#| include: false
# 28) 90% PI for SF forecasts on test
sf_sd_test <- sapply(forecast_res_test$Q, function(Sig) sqrt(Sig[1,1]))
z90 <- qnorm(0.95)
sf_lower_test <- sf_fore_test - z90 * sf_sd_test
sf_upper_test <- sf_fore_test + z90 * sf_sd_test

# 29) Plot last 60 days of training (before Dec 2024), actual Dec 2024, and Dec 2024 forecasts for SF

# Determine indices for last 60 training days:
last_train_date <- as.Date("2024-11-30")
last_train_idx <- which(all_dates == last_train_date)
start_plot <- max(1, last_train_idx - 59)

# Data frame for last 60 days of training (observed)
train_last60_df <- tibble(
  date = all_dates[start_plot:last_train_idx],
  temp = sf_true_train[start_plot:length(train_idx)],
  type = "Observed (Train)"
)

# Data frame for actual December 2024
dec2024_dates <- all_dates[test_idx]
actual_dec_df <- tibble(
  date = dec2024_dates,
  temp = sf_true_test,
  type = "Observed (Dec 2024)"
)

# Data frame for December 2024 forecasts
forecast_dec_df <- tibble(
  date = dec2024_dates,
  temp = sf_fore_test,
  type = "Forecast (Dec 2024)"
)

# Combine all three
plot_df <- bind_rows(train_last60_df, actual_dec_df, forecast_dec_df)

# 30) Plot everything with 90% prediction interval for Dec 2024
ribbon_dec_df <- tibble(
  date  = dec2024_dates,
  lower = sf_lower_test,
  upper = sf_upper_test
)

library(ggplot2)
ggplot() +
  geom_ribbon(data = ribbon_dec_df,
              aes(x = date, ymin = lower, ymax = upper),
              fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.2) +
  geom_line(data = plot_df,
            aes(x = date, y = temp, color = type, linetype = type),
            size = 0.8) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      "Observed (Train)"     = "orange",
      "Observed (Dec 2024)"  = "black",
      "Forecast (Dec 2024)"  = "steelblue"
    )
  ) +
  scale_linetype_manual(
    values = c(
      "Observed (Train)"     = "solid",
      "Observed (Dec 2024)"  = "solid",
      "Forecast (Dec 2024)"  = "dashed"
    )
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = "SF Tmin: Spatial‐Factor DLM — Training vs. December 2024",
    subtitle = sprintf("Train RMSE=%.2f, MAE=%.2f | Test RMSE=%.2f, MAE=%.2f",
                       rmse_train, mae_train, rmse_test, mae_test),
    x        = "Date",
    y        = "Min Temperature (°C)",
    color    = "",
    linetype = ""
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(
    xlim = c(all_dates[start_plot], dec2024_dates[length(dec2024_dates)]),
    ylim = range(
      c(train_last60_df$temp, actual_dec_df$temp, ribbon_dec_df$lower, ribbon_dec_df$upper),
      na.rm = TRUE
    )
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```


```{r}
#| eval: false
#| include: false
#checkresiduals
# (Assumes Y_train, filter_res_train, sf_hat_train, and all_dates/train_idx are already defined)

# 1) Extract in‐sample residuals for each station
resid_matrix <- Y_train - filter_res_train$f   # [n_train × 4]
colnames(resid_matrix) <- station_names

# 2) Convert residuals to ts objects (optional)
dates_train <- all_dates[train_idx]
resid_ts <- lapply(1:ncol(resid_matrix), function(j) {
  ts(resid_matrix[, j],
     start = c(year(dates_train[1]), yday(dates_train[1])),
     frequency = 365)
})
names(resid_ts) <- station_names

# 3) Choose SF residuals for diagnostics
sf_resid <- resid_matrix[, "SAN FRANCISCO DWTN"]

# 4) Time‐series plot of SF residuals
plot(dates_train, sf_resid, type = "l", col = "darkgray",
     main = "SF In‐Sample Residuals over Time",
     xlab = "Date", ylab = "Residual (°C)")

# 5) Histogram + density overlay
hist(sf_resid, breaks = 30, freq = FALSE,
     main = "Histogram of SF Residuals (Train)",
     xlab = "Residual (°C)", col = "lightblue")
lines(density(sf_resid, na.rm = TRUE), col = "darkblue", lwd = 2)

# 6) QQ‐plot vs Normal
qqnorm(sf_resid, main = "QQ‐Plot of SF Residuals (Train)")
qqline(sf_resid, col = "red", lwd = 2)

# 7) ACF and PACF of SF residuals
par(mar = c(4, 4, 2, 1))
acf(sf_resid, na.action = na.pass, main = "ACF of SF Residuals")
pacf(sf_resid, na.action = na.pass, main = "PACF of SF Residuals")
par(mfrow = c(1,1))

# 8) Ljung-Box test for autocorrelation at various lags
for (L in c(7, 14, 21, 28)) {
  lb <- Box.test(sf_resid, lag = L, type = "Ljung-Box", fitdf = 0)
  cat(sprintf("Ljung-Box (lag=%d):  statistic=%.3f, p‐value=%.3f\n",
              L, lb$statistic, lb$p.value))
}

# 9) Kolmogorov-Smirnov test for normality (since Shapiro‐Wilk fails for large n)
sf_resid2 <- na.omit(sf_resid)
ks <- ks.test(sf_resid2, "pnorm",
              mean = mean(sf_resid2),
              sd   = sd(sf_resid2))
cat(sprintf("KS test: statistic=%.3f, p‐value=%.3f\n", ks$statistic, ks$p.value))

# 10) Residuals vs Fitted plot
sf_fitted <- sf_hat_train
valid_idx_train <- which(!is.na(sf_resid))
plot(sf_fitted[valid_idx_train], sf_resid[valid_idx_train],
     xlab = "Fitted Value (°C)", ylab = "Residual (°C)",
     main = "SF Residuals vs Fitted", col = "darkgray", pch = 16)
abline(h = 0, col = "red", lwd = 2)

# 11) Repeat ACF/PACF and Ljung-Box for each station
for (j in seq_along(station_names)) {
  stn <- station_names[j]
  resid_j <- resid_matrix[, j]
  cat("\n--- Diagnostics for station:", stn, "---\n")
  acf(resid_j, na.action = na.pass, main = paste("ACF of", stn, "Residuals"))
  pacf(resid_j, na.action = na.pass, main = paste("PACF of", stn, "Residuals"))
  lbj <- Box.test(resid_j, lag = 14, type = "Ljung-Box", fitdf = 0)
  cat(sprintf("Ljung-Box (lag=14) for %s: statistic=%.3f, p‐value=%.3f\n",
              stn, lbj$statistic, lbj$p.value))
}

# 12) Summary statistics for each station’s residuals
for (j in seq_along(station_names)) {
  stn <- station_names[j]
  resid_j <- resid_matrix[, j]
  cat(sprintf("\nStation %s: mean=%.4f, sd=%.4f, missing%%=%.2f\n",
              stn,
              mean(resid_j, na.rm = TRUE),
              sd(resid_j, na.rm = TRUE),
              100 * sum(is.na(resid_j)) / length(resid_j)))
}

# 13) Residual heatmap (optional)
library(reshape2)
resid_df <- data.frame(date = dates_train, resid_matrix)
resid_melt <- melt(resid_df, id.vars = "date", variable.name = "Station", value.name = "Residual")
ggplot(resid_melt, aes(x = date, y = Station, fill = Residual)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "blue", mid = "white", high = "red", midpoint = 0) +
  labs(title = "Residuals Heatmap (Train)", x = "Date", y = "Station") +
  theme_minimal()

```
